title: Telosian Jedi Academy
content:
{{Top|ffa|leg}}
{{Structure
|image=[[File:Telos_polar.jpg]]
|name=Telosian Jedi Academy
|constructed=Between [[3958 BBY]] and c. [[3956 BBY]]<ref name="Construction date">According to [[Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force (real-life book)|''Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force'']], the Telosian [[Jedi academy|Jedi Academy]] was in existence around [[3956 BBY]]. Additionally, ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]'' states that the Academy was constructed after the [[bombing of Telos IV]], which is dated to [[3958 BBY]] by ''[[Knights of the Old Republic Campaign Guide]]''. Therefore, the Telosian Academy must have been constructed between 3958 and around 3956 [[Galactic Standard Calendar|BBY]].</ref>
|destroyed=
|type=
|location=[[Telos IV]]<ref name="KOTOR CG">''[[Knights of the Old Republic Campaign Guide]]''</ref>
|interest=
|rebuilt=
|builder=[[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Atris]]<ref name="TSL">''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]''</ref>
|height=
|width=
|affiliation=*[[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]]<ref name="TSL" />
*[[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]<ref name="TSL" />
*[[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]<ref name="BHC">''[[The Bounty Hunter Code: From the Files of Boba Fett]]''</ref>
*[[Bounty Hunters' Guild/Legends|Bounty Hunter's Guild]]<ref name="BHC" />
}}
{{Quote|Yes, it bears the semblance of an academy… but where are all the students? Curious.|Kreia comments on the Telosian Jedi Academy|<ref name="TSL" />}}
The '''Telosian Jedi Academy''', also known as the '''Telos Polar Academy''', was founded by the [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Atris]] during the [[Jedi Civil War]], in the wake of [[Darth Revan's Sith Empire]]'s [[Attack on Dantooine (Jedi Civil War)|attack]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Dantooine/Legends|Dantooine]] that left the [[Jedi Enclave]] there in ruins. Hidden inside a mesa in the polar region of the planet [[Telos IV]] that housed the abandoned [[Telos Polar Irrigation System]], the [[Jedi academy|Jedi Academy]] was afforded secrecy and security. Although the Academy never housed any students, it was inhabited by Atris and the [[Echani/Legends|Echani]] [[Handmaiden Sisters]] who served [[Gender/Legends|her]]. The building also hosted numerous [[Sith holocron/Legends|Sith holocrons]] that Atris had collected, along with other relics. Among the collection was various materials of learning that had been saved from Dantooine's Jedi Enclave.

[[3951 BBY|Five years after]] the end of the Civil War, [[Meetra Surik]], an [[Exile (punishment)/Legends|exiled]] [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] who had been fleeing the [[Sith Triumvirate|Sith]], traveled to Telos' [[Citadel Station]] aboard the ''[[Ebon Hawk]]''. When Atris had the [[Dynamic-class freighter/Legends|ship]] brought to the Academy, Surik, accompanied by her companions, [[Kreia]], [[Atton Rand]], and [[Bao-Dur]], followed a trail of evidence that pinpointed the ''Hawk''{{'s}} location, thereby discovering the Academy. Once there, Surik met with Atris to discuss the past struggles between the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] and the Sith before leaving to [[Dark Wars|gather]] other [[Lost Jedi|surviving Jedi Masters]].

==Description==
[[File:Telos_academy_map.jpg|thumb|left|240px|A map of the Academy]]
Located within a mesa in the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Telos IV]]'s polar region that housed the abandoned [[Telos Polar Irrigation System]], the Telosian Jedi Academy was retrofitted from the facility by [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Atris]]—founder of the Academy and [[Jedi historian]] of the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]]. Also known as the Telos Polar Academy, the building was designed to be completely self-contained, having been built with every facility needed to maintain its self-sufficiency. The [[Jedi academy|Academy's]] interior was clad in [[Color/Legends|blue-gray]] hues, and was similar in appearance to the [[Water/Legends|water]]-distribution network of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] [[Capital/Legends|capital]] planet [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]].<ref name="TSL" />

The Academy's mesa drew power from the Telosian shield network's power grid to [[Deflector shield generator/Legends|generate]] a [[Deflector shield/Legends|shield]] protecting itself. The building held a [[Hangar/Legends|hangar]] that was large enough to accommodate a [[Light freighter/Legends|light freighter]]. In addition, it had storage rooms, a [[Prison/Legends|prison]], and separate quarters for any on-site personnel, as well as a private meditation chamber for Atris, as well as a [[Jedi High Council/Legends|Jedi Council]] meeting chamber. The chamber was circular, with seats resting upon yellow and red carpets and arranged at equal distances from each other around a gray rock pillar. The mesa was black and squat, with its base wider than its top, the [[Polar Plateau]], which was covered with snow. The Academy's entrance was located at the plateau's southern half. Four black towers rose up around the mesa, arranged at equal distances from each other to form a perimeter.<ref name="TSL" />

In [[990 BBY]],<ref name="TJPdate">{{Blogspot|subdomain=geekosity|url=2010/12/endnotes-for-star-wars-jedi-path-part-1.html|name=Daniel Wallace's Geekosity|text=Endnotes for Star Wars: The Jedi Path (part 1 of 4)|quote=Okay, so we've established that this particular copy of the book dates from 115 BBY. But the content inside is […] dating from the year 990 BBY.}} — The content of [[The Jedi Path: A Manual for Students of the Force (real-life book)|''The Jedi Path: A Manual for Students of the Force'']] dates to [[990 BBY]].</ref> Telos was a Jedi world and a hub of information on which [[Jedi Consular]]s fulfilled their researching and [[Teacher/Legends|teaching]] duties. The training academy on Telos specialized in data collection and analysis at the [[Time/Legends|time]], and had input locks that were the envy of [[Scholar/Legends|scholars]]. However, they were only accessible to Jedi.<ref name="TJP">[[The Jedi Path: A Manual for Students of the Force (real-life book)|''The Jedi Path: A Manual for Students of the Force'']]</ref>

==History==
===Origins===
Constructed under strictest secrecy after Telos was [[Bombing of Telos IV|bombarded]] from [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]] by [[Revan/Legends|Darth Revan's]] [[Darth Revan's Sith Empire|Sith Empire]]<ref name="TSL" /> in [[3958 BBY]],<ref name="KOTOR CG" /> the Academy was intended to be a point of retreat for the Jedi Order should the [[Jedi Enclave]] on [[Dantooine/Legends|Dantooine]] ever fall. Telos was selected to host the Academy, partly in the hope that the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] would not devastate the same world twice; however, prior to the war it had served as a place where [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] who had failed their [[Jedi training/Legends|training]] would continue to serve [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] as [[Farm/Legends|farmers]] and laborers. Throughout the [[Jedi Civil War]], which Revan's Sith waged against the Jedi and the Galactic Republic<ref name="TSL" /> from [[3959 BBY]]<ref name="TORE">''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic Encyclopedia]]''</ref> to [[3956 BBY]],<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> Jedi [[Holocron/Legends|holocrons]] and other materials and relics of historical value had been secretly evacuated from Dantooine to Telos.<ref name="TSL" /> However, according to Jedi [[Historian/Legends|historian]] [[Tionne Solusar]] in ''[[Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force]]'', a historical compendium created in [[40 ABY/Legends|40 ABY]], the Jedi Master and historian Atris also amassed a collection of [[Sith holocron/Legends|Sith holocrons]] at the Academy around 3956 BBY.<ref name="JvS">[[Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force (real-life book)|''Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force'']]</ref> without the knowledge of other Jedi, delving into forbidden knowledge of [[The Force/Legends|the Force's]] [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]] in hopes of finding a way to defeat the Sith from within.<ref name="TSL" />

[[File:LostJedi-KOTOR2.jpg|thumb|right|280px|Jedi Masters at the Dantooine Jedi Enclave]]
At the height of the Jedi Civil War<ref name="TSL" /> in 3956 BBY,<ref name="NEC" /> the [[Dantooine Jedi Enclave Council]] foresaw the destruction of the Enclave and organized an evacuation. While the warning came too late, a number of senior Jedi Masters and [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Knights]] escaped the oncoming [[Attack on Dantooine (Jedi Civil War)|attack]] by Revan's Sith Empire and helped Atris spirit a number of Jedi artifacts to Telos.<ref name="TSL" />

===Administration under Atris===
During the [[First Jedi Purge|Jedi purge]] that followed the war, Atris forsook the other Jedi, blaming them for their apathy and unwillingness to actively fight the threat posed by the Sith. With most members of the Jedi Order being [[Death/Legends|killed]] at the disastrous [[Conclave at Katarr|conclave]] on [[Katarr/Legends|Katarr]]<ref name="TSL" /> around [[3952 BBY]],<ref name="Katarr">[[Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force (real-life book)|''Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force'']] states that the [[Conclave at Katarr]] took place one [[Standard year/Legends|year]] before the approximate date [[3951 BBY]]. Therefore, the event occurred around [[3952 BBY]].</ref> Atris decided to bring about the Order's rebirth. She gathered several members of the [[Echani/Legends|Echani]] [[Species/Legends|species]], [[Reproduction/Legends|daughters]] of the famed Echani [[General/Legends|General]] [[Yusanis]] and collectively known as the [[Handmaiden Sisters]], at the Academy, and demanded an oath from each that they would never be trained in the Jedi ways, even if they themselves were [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]].

Atris then instructed them in vigorous mental exercises that were aimed at teaching them to resist Force-based influences. The Handmaidens were intended to protect the Academy from the influence of the dark side; as part of their service they were ordered to deal with any Jedi who showed signs of falling under its sway, including killing them. Atris was the only one whom the Handmaidens absolutely trusted; she was the only Jedi who resided within the Academy. The Jedi historian never trained any apprentices in the ways of the Force while there, as almost nobody knew of the place's existence.<ref name="TSL" />

===Exiled Jedi===
{{Quote|You've got to be joking. What is a Jedi Academy doing out here in the middle of nowhere?|Atton Rand, upon arriving at the Academy|<ref name="TSL" />}}
Furthermore, Atris herself had never before taken on a [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]], as [[Meetra Surik]] pointed out to one of the Sisters when she arrived at the Academy. While the surviving members of the Jedi Order knew of the secret training facility, they presumed that Atris had been killed and that the Academy had fallen into disrepair.<ref name="TSL" />

[[File:Telos_academy.jpg|thumb|left|320px|The Council chamber and a catwalk to the meditation chamber]]
In 3651 BBY, a Handmaiden Sister stole from Telos' [[Citadel Station]] the ''[[Ebon Hawk]]'', the [[Dynamic-class freighter/Legends|ship]] used the [[Exile (punishment)/Legends|exiled]] Jedi [[Meetra Surik]]. The ''Hawk'' was taken to Telos, where the Academy's hangar secretly received the [[Freighter/Legends|freighter]]. From [[Restoration Zone]] [[RZ-0031]] on Telos, [[Engineer/Legends|engineer]] [[Bao-Dur]] [[Skirmish on Telos|accessed]] a landing pad's [[Computer/Legends|computer]] panel to help Surik find<ref name="TSL" /> her<ref name="Exile">''[[The New Essential Guide to Droids]]'' states that [[Meetra Surik]] was [[Gender/Legends|female]] and uses she/her pronouns.</ref> lost [[Starship/Legends|ship]]. While the Jedi Exile said that the [[Telos Security Force]] could only determine that the ''Hawk'' was not present at any of Telos' sanctioned landing sites, Bao-Dur deduced that the ship could be at a shielded yet unofficial site. The engineer then found that power was being diverted to supply a shield covering a small mesa in the polar region, and confirmed a subtle, seemingly unsuspicious anomaly in the planetary shield network's power grid. While an [[Orbit/Legends|orbital]] [[Camera/Legends|camera]] revealed empty lands, the Exile's [[Teacher/Legends|mentor]], [[Kreia]], stated that they should investigate further, feeling that the lead was their best chance at finding the ''Hawk''. The group thus took a [[Ministry-class orbital shuttle|shuttle]] from the nearby [[Telosian Military Base|military base]] and traveled to the polar region.<ref name="TSL" />

While passing through the snowing polar region, the [[Shuttle/Legends|shuttle]] was shot down by pursuing [[HK-50 series assassin droid]]s and crashed on the Polar Plateau. While the Exile and her group survived the crash, Bao-Dur was rendered unconscious, and the crew were confronted by three HK-50 units. Having detected nothing of interest at the icy mesa, the droids queried why the Exile traveled to the remnants of the Telos Irrigation System before attacking her. However, the Exile and her group destroyed the HK-50s and proceeded to search for refuge, leaving Bao-Dur behind as they entered the secret Academy's entrance.<ref name="TSL" />

While two Echani Sisters inspected the crash site, the Exile's group arrived at the Academy's entrance chamber below and were confronted by three other Sisters.<ref name="TSL" />

Surik retrieved the ''[[Ebon Hawk]]'', which had been stolen by one of the Handmaidens—however, upon securing the vessel, she immediately left Telos and Atris behind. Even when the three surviving Jedi Masters, [[Zez-Kai Ell]], [[Kavar]], [[Vrook Lamar]], learned that Atris was still alive and preparing to restore the Order, the Telosian Academy proved to be of little significance to them or their plans. Instead of resurrecting the Order on Telos, they decided to meet in the rebuilt Jedi Enclave on Dantooine in the hope of fighting the threat of the Sith when it was finally exposed.<ref name="TSL" />

[[File:Telos_entrance.jpg|thumb|right|280px|The Academy entrance]]
Atris, however, was proven to be wrong in her belief that the Sith would not attack Telos twice. Darth Nihilus arrived with his fleet above Telos in order to feed on the Force energy within the Jedi who he believed dwelled at the Academy. Nihilus's plans, however, were thwarted by Surik, who defeated the Sith Lord aboard his [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]], the [[Ravager (Centurion-class)|''Ravager'']], thus preserving the Academy's structural integrity. By then, as part of the training of Meetra Surik, Kreia had exposed Atris's corruption and fall to the dark side, provoking the Jedi Master into acknowledging her own dark nature before leaving for [[Malachor V]] to complete Surik's training. Soon after, fresh from her efforts to [[Battle of Telos IV|drive]] the Sith away, Surik arrived at the Academy and was [[Duel in the Telosian Jedi Academy|attacked]] by Atris in the Academy's Council chambers. However, Atris was defeated, and, against her predictions, was spared by Meetra Surik in the wake of their [[Lightsaber duel/Legends|duel]]. Seeing the flaws of her actions as a result, Atris decided to leave the Jedi, wishing to find her true self in doing so. Atris then revealed to Surik the location of Kreia, directing the exiled Jedi to confront her mentor on Malachor V whilst she herself remained within her chamber of Sith holocrons.<ref name="TSL" />

===The new Jedi Order===
{{Quote|Some of the civil-war–era holocrons remain on Telos, from the old Academy. I convinced Master Yoda it was time to bring the last of them to Coruscant.|Jedi Master Tahl|<ref name="DS1" />}}
As instituted by Kreia, a new Jedi Order was later formed by Surik and her followers,<ref name="TSL" /> and the Telosian Academy continued to be maintained during the [[Cold War (Galactic Republic–Sith Empire)|Cold War]] between the Republic and the [[Sith Empire (Post–Great Hyperspace War)|resurgent Sith Empire]].<ref name="Crew Skill">{{TORcite|type=crewskill|Archaelogy|The Telosian Academy}}</ref>

{{Gamemechanics|Star Wars: The Old Republic}}
Around [[3643 BBY]],<ref name="TORdate">Per the reasoning [[Template:TOR_dates|here]], Act I of ''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic]]'' takes place around [[3643 BBY]]. Since players of ''The Old Republic'' can assign their companions on the mission "The Telosian Academy" independently of the main class storyline at any point during the [[Star Wars video games|game]], the mission must take place at some point around 3643 [[Galactic Standard Calendar|BBY]].</ref> an individual affiliated with one of those factions dispatched a companion to Telos IV to [[Archaeology/Legends|explore]] the secrets of the Academy, and the facility's caretakers permitted the party to study the building in return for a donation. With help from the caretakers, the crew uncovered a Jedi weapon that had been created by Atris.<ref name="Crew Skill" />
{{Endgame}}

[[File:Telos_libraries.jpg|thumb|left|224px|The last of the holocrons from the Telosian Academy were retrieved from Telos IV by Jedi Master Tahl.]]
In 990 BBY,<ref name="TJPdate" /> the structure was used by the Jedi Order as a satellite training academy that specialized in data collection and analysis. Alongside the [[Kamparas Jedi Training Center|Kamparas]] and [[Jedi Academy (Obroa-skai)|Obroa-skai]] Jedi academies, the Telosian Academy was recommended to [[Jedi Initiate/Legends|Jedi Initiates]] with an affinity for [[Science/Legends|science]] by [[Jedi ace]] and New Sith Wars veteran [[Crix Sunburris]] in his writings in the Jedi [[Book/Legends|guidebook]] ''[[The Jedi Path: A Manual for Students of the Force]]''.<ref name="TJP" /> However, by [[53 BBY/Legends|53 BBY]],<ref name="DSdate">{{Marvel|url=comics/issue/54280/star_wars_jedi_-_the_dark_side_2011_1|text=Star Wars: Jedi - The Dark Side (2011) #1}} dates the events of [[Jedi — The Dark Side 1|''Jedi—The Dark Side'' 1]] to twenty-one [[Standard year/Legends|years]] before the events in [[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''The Phantom Menace'']], which ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates to [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]]. Thus, it could be found that the events of ''Jedi—The Dark Side 1'' took place in [[53 BBY/Legends|53 BBY]].</ref> the Telosian Jedi Academy was no longer operational; Jedi Master [[Tahl]] convinced [[Grand Master/Legends|Grand Master]] [[Yoda/Legends|Yoda]] that the last of the holocrons from the time of the Jedi Civil War be finally brought from the "old Academy" to the Coruscant Jedi Temple.<ref name="DS1">[[Jedi — The Dark Side 1|''Jedi — The Dark Side'' 1]]</ref> Per Tahl's request, she accompanied fellow Jedi [[Qui-Gon Jinn/Legends|Qui-Gon Jinn]] on his [[Mission to Telos (53 BBY)|assignment]] to Telos IV, where the [[Noorian]] retrieved the Telosian Academy's holocrons from an [[Library/Legends|archive]] on the planet with temperature-controlled packing.<ref name="DS2">[[Jedi — The Dark Side 2|''Jedi — The Dark Side'' 2]]</ref> Tahl then left for the [[Landor system/Legends|Landor system]] alone to rescue [[Stieg Wa]] from [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]] at the behest of Master Yoda.<ref name="DS4">[[Jedi — The Dark Side 4|''Jedi — The Dark Side'' 4]]</ref>

===Fall to the Sith===
{{Quote|The Jedi were mistaken if they thought they could hide these academy worlds, as if this very volume didn't reveal their location. My legions wiped them out, saving us a great deal of time tracking down the Jedi children.|Darth Sidious, ''The Jedi Path: A Manual for Students of the Force''|<ref name="TJP" />}}
The Sith Lord [[Palpatine/Legends|Darth Sidious]] noted in his stolen copy of ''The Jedi Path: A Manual for Students of the Force'' that the Telosian facility was one of many Jedi sites he had uncovered during the [[Great Jedi Purge/Legends|Great Jedi Purge]]. Sidious remarked that the book itself revealed the academy's location, saving the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] time as they hunted Jedi [[Youngling/Legends|younglings]].<ref name="TJP" /> Later, by special arrangement with the organization, the Empire allowed members of the [[Bounty Hunters' Guild/Legends|Bounty Hunter's Guild]] to train on three properties formerly used as [[Jedi chapter house/Legends|Jedi chapter houses]]. The three facilities' locations were a closely guarded secret, being known only to Guild members and a select faction of [[Imperial officer/Legends|Imperial officers]]. As annotated by [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]] in his book ''[[The Bounty Hunter Code]]'', specifically his copy of the ''[[Bounty Hunters Guild Handbook]]''{{'s}} Imperial edition, the locations were Telos, [[Kamparas]], and [[Bogden 3]].<ref name="BHC" />

==Inhabitants==
[[File:Atrisheadshot.jpg|thumb|right|150px|Jedi Master Atris, the Academy's founder]]
Although the Jedi Master Atris wished to one day train Jedi at the Academy, it was only inhabited by herself and six young Echani warrior-women who were collectively known as the Handmaiden Sisters. It was here that Atris secluded herself after the disaster at the Conclave on Katarr, during which over a hundred of the remaining members of the Jedi Order were killed by Darth Nihilus when he sucked all life from the planet in order to feed his hunger for Force-energy. While Atris meditated with her large collection of Sith holocrons in an attempt to unlock the secrets of defeating the Sith, the Handmaiden Sisters relentlessly trained themselves both in fighting techniques and in closing their minds to Force-based influences. This resulted in them being almost Force-blind, unable to use it even if they were Force-sensitive.<ref name="TSL" />

==Behind the scenes==
===Notable appearances===
The Telosian Jedi Academy was created by [[Obsidian Entertainment]] for their [[2004]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]], ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]''.<ref name="TSL" /> The Jedi Academy was later mentioned in ''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic]]'', [[BioWare]]'s [[2011]] massively multiplayer online roleplaying game, in which the academy was a possible location for players to assign their companions to in the optional quest "Telosian Jedi Academy." That mission was eventually removed from the game.<ref name="Crew Skill" />

===Alternate storylines===
If the player chooses the [[Canon|non-canonical]] dark side path in ''The Sith Lords'', there are different conversation choices with the Academy's denizens during the first visit, and again when Surik returns to the Academy in the wake of the Battle of Telos IV. During her battle with Atris, Surik can choose to kill the Academy founder or save her from the dark side of the Force, before learning of Kreia's intentions and departing for Malachor V.<ref name="TSL" />

There are also some differences with the game and, hence, the fate of the Academy itself if the player chooses the non-canonical path of a male Meetra Surik. In it, prior to Surik's arrival at the Academy in the wake of the Battle of Telos IV, the former Handmaiden Sister [[Brianna]] arrives. Enraged, Atris duels her, eventually subduing the Handmaiden with [[Force lightning/Legends|Force lightning]] just as Surik arrives to confront the former Jedi Master. At the end of the game, Kreia still gives a number of predictions, however instead of predicting a future for Mical, she instead states that Brianna will take up Atris' post of Jedi Historian.<ref name="TSL" />
